title: linanthus bigelovii subsp. johnsonii
intro: Linanthus bigelovii subsp. johnsonii, also known as Johnson's linanthus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the phlox family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds found in its leaves and flowers. Johnson's linanthus has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and acting as an antioxidant. It is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, allowing the plant's bioactive compounds to be extracted and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
